title: Structures at infinity in the character variety
speaker: Carlos Simpson
affiliation: University of Nice; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: We consider compactifications of the Betti, de Rham and Dolbeault realizations of the character variety. Starting from an example, we look at what can be said, mostly conjecturally, about the relationship between these spaces.

proof_stages.label: Character variety as an affine quotient
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines the Betti character variety Hom(pi_1(X), SL_r(C))//SL_r(C), explains its affine GIT quotient structure via invariant trace functions, and frames the central problem as understanding its noncompact directions at infinity.

proof_stages.label: Nonabelian Hodge comparison spaces
proof_stages.summary: The de Rham moduli space of flat algebraic connections and the Dolbeault/Higgs moduli space are introduced, together with the Riemann-Hilbert analytic isomorphism and the Hitchin-equation homeomorphism relating them to the character variety.

proof_stages.label: Deligne-Hodge interpolation and compactification
proof_stages.summary: Lambda-connections are used to form the Hodge moduli space over A^1, interpolating between connections and Higgs bundles, and the C* action plus the Hitchin map gives a compactification whose boundary is a quotient of the non-nilpotent Higgs locus, often smooth in an orbifold sense.

proof_stages.label: Reduction to large-parameter connections
proof_stages.summary: To probe the Riemann-Hilbert correspondence near the compactifying divisor, the speaker restricts to a transverse curve and rescales it into a family of connections of the form nabla_t = nabla_0 + t phi, whose monodromy is described by transport matrices on the universal cover.

proof_stages.label: Building-valued limits and transport exponents
proof_stages.summary: Using the Morgan-Shalen-Parreau compactification philosophy, rescaled symmetric spaces converge through an ultrafilter to an R-building, and the limiting distance between images of two points is the exponent lim (1/t) log ||T_{p,q}(t)|| of WKB transport growth.

proof_stages.label: Spectral data and the local WKB differential theorem
proof_stages.summary: For the Higgs field phi, the eigenvalue one-forms define the spectral curve, and classical local WKB estimates along noncritical paths prove that the limiting harmonic map has differential given by the real parts of these eigenforms.

proof_stages.label: Universal prebuilding factorization conjecture
proof_stages.summary: The conjectural next step is that for generic spectral data there is a universal map from the universal cover to a prebuilding such that every building-valued harmonic map with the prescribed differential factors uniquely through it by a distance-preserving map.

proof_stages.label: Genericity via spectral networks and saddle connections
proof_stages.summary: The required genericity is formulated as absence of BPS states or saddle connections in the associated spectral network, with higher-rank collisions producing the generalized compact trajectories expected to define the nongeneric walls.

examples.name: The four-punctured projective line
examples.note: The speaker mentions X = P^1 minus four points as a concrete Riemann-surface case of the character-variety setup, without using it in the argument developed in this lecture.
examples.engagement_reason: The four-punctured projective line is named as the intended example, but the speaker never carries out any calculation or construction on it.

examples.name: Projective-space quotient analogy
examples.note: The compactification by deleting the nilpotent cone and quotienting by the C* action is compared to constructing projective space from affine space minus the origin modulo scalars.
examples.engagement_reason: Projective space is invoked only as an analogy for taking affine space minus the origin modulo a C^* action.

examples.name: SL2 quadratic differential leaf space
examples.note: In the classical SL2 case, a quadratic differential has an R-tree of leaves, illustrating the kind of universal prebuilding the higher-rank conjecture seeks to generalize.

examples.name: Higher-rank spectral-network collision
examples.note: The collision of an ij trajectory with a jk trajectory to create an ik trajectory is described as the observation from Gaiotto-Moore-Neitzke spectral networks that motivated the project’s higher-rank notion of saddle connection.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker works through the higher-rank collision picture, labeling ij and jk foliation lines, explaining their collision into an ik line, and drawing a more complicated network.
